Errors propagate by default — there is no silent unwind."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq)] pub enum Outcome { # [doc = " Success."] Ok (Value) , # [doc = " An explicit, propagating error."] Err (StructuredError) , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The outcome of handling: an error is **either recovered** (an explicit value with an honest"] # [doc = " guarantee) **or re-propagated** (an explicit error — possibly transformed). There is deliberately"] # [doc = " **no \"dropped\" variant**: never-silent (I1) is a property of this type, so a mutant handler that"] # [doc = " discarded an error could not be expressed here."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq)] pub enum Resolution { # [doc = " Recovered with an explicit value, honestly tagged (a substituted fallback is `Declared` — I2)."] Recovered { # [doc = " The recovered value."] value : Value , # [doc = " Its honest guarantee (downgrade-only; never upgraded — VR-5)."] guarantee : GuaranteeStrength , # [doc = " The `PolicyRef` of the policy that recovered it, if any."] policy : Option < ContentHash > , } , # [doc = " Re-propagated — the error continues to surface (additive over the explicit error — I1)."] Propagated { # [doc = " The propagating error (possibly transformed/escalated)."] error : StructuredError , # [doc = " The `PolicyRef` of the policy that acted, if any."] policy : Option < ContentHash > , } , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Handle an [`Outcome`] under a reified recovery `policy`, drawing on a budget ledger and an"] # [doc = " `attempt` thunk (re-run for `retry`). The result is **always** [`Resolution::Recovered`] or"] # [doc = " [`Resolution::Propagated`] — never a drop (I1):"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - `Ok(v)` passes through as `Recovered` carrying the value's own guarantee (nothing was recovered)."] # [doc = " - `Err(e)` with **no** matching rule re-propagates `e` **unchanged** (I1)."] # [doc = " - `fallback` recovers with an honest `Declared` value (I2/VR-5)."] # [doc = " - `retry(<=N)` re-attempts up to `N` times; if all fail the **original error propagates** (additive)."] # [doc = " - `escalate(c')` re-propagates a transformed error (still explicit)."] # [doc = " - `cleanup_then_propagate` runs a **bounded** effect (a budget overrun is a graceful"] # [doc = "   `EffectBudgetExhausted`, swallowed *for the cleanup only*) and then propagates the original error."] # [must_use] pub fn handle (outcome : Outcome , policy : & RecoveryPolicy , budgets : & mut Budgets , mut attempt : impl FnMut () -> Outcome ,) -> Resolution { let error = match outcome { Outcome :: Ok (value) => { let guarantee = value . meta () . guarantee () ; return Resolution :: Recovered { value , guarantee , policy : None , } ; } Outcome :: Err (e) => e , } ; let Some (action) = policy . action_for (& error . class) else { return Resolution :: Propagated { error , policy : None , } ; } ; let pref = Some (policy . content_id ()) ; match action { RecoveryAction :: Fallback { value } => Resolution :: Recovered { value : value . as_ref () . clone () , guarantee : GuaranteeStrength :: Declared , policy : pref , } , RecoveryAction :: Retry { max_attempts } => { for _ in 0 .. * max_attempts { if let Outcome :: Ok (value) = attempt () { let guarantee = value . meta () . guarantee () ; return Resolution :: Recovered { value , guarantee , policy : pref , } ; } } Resolution :: Propagated { error , policy : pref , } } RecoveryAction :: Escalate { to } => Resolution :: Propagated { error : StructuredError { class : to . clone () , message : format ! ("escalated from {}: {}" , error . class , error . message) , site : error . site , } , policy : pref , } , RecoveryAction :: CleanupThenPropagate { effect } => { let _ = budgets . consume (effect . clone () , 1) ; Resolution :: Propagated { error , policy : pref , } } } }
